prompt: Add a circuit breaker pattern implementation with closed, open, and half-open states

turns.content:
<task>
Add a circuit breaker pattern implementation with closed, open, and half-open states
</task>

arguments.summary: Empty circuitbreaker package. Will build CircuitBreaker with three states: closed (normal), open (failing), half-open (testing).

next_steps.description: Create pkg/circuitbreaker/breaker.go with State enum, CircuitBreaker struct tracking failure count, last failure time, and state transitions
Add Execute(fn) method that runs function if circuit allows, transitions states based on success/failure, test with mock failures

turns.content:
=== RUN   TestClosedOnSuccess
--- PASS: TestClosedOnSuccess (0.00s)
=== RUN   TestOpensAfterMaxFailures
--- PASS: TestOpensAfterMaxFailures (0.00s)
=== RUN   TestHalfOpenAfterTimeout
--- PASS: TestHalfOpenAfterTimeout (0.06s)
PASS

arguments.summary: Created CircuitBreaker with three states: Closed (normal), Open (rejects with ErrCircuitOpen), HalfOpen (test single request after timeout). Thread-safe with mutex. 3 tests pass: success stays closed, failures open circuit, timeout transitions to half-open.
arguments.final_answer:
Created pkg/circuitbreaker/breaker.go:
- State enum: Closed, Open, HalfOpen
- Execute(fn) runs function if circuit allows
- Opens after maxFailures consecutive errors
- Returns ErrCircuitOpen when open
- Transitions to HalfOpen after resetTimeout
- Resets to Closed on successful half-open execution
- Thread-safe with sync.Mutex
- 3 tests passing
